enrichment.short_description: White-label platform for creators and brands to launch branded iOS, Android, and web community apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Disciple lets creators and brands launch fully white-label iOS, Android, and web community apps with courses, livestreaming, and analytics.

features.title: Branded mobile and web apps
features.description: Launch a fully white-label iOS, Android, and web app under your own brand, listed in the app stores.

features.title: Groups and livestreaming
features.description: Organize members into groups with in-app messaging and multi-host livestreaming on higher tiers.

features.title: Course monetization
features.description: Sell monetizable courses with video hosting allowances up to 20,000 minutes on the Pro plan.

features.title: Content library
features.description: Centralize resources and content so members can access materials inside the branded app.

features.title: Member analytics
features.description: Track engagement with standard analytics on entry plans or advanced analytics on higher tiers.

features.title: Integrations and API
features.description: Connect with Zapier and Mailchimp, with API access available starting on the Pro plan.

pros: Fully white-label on higher tiers, with no mention of Disciple branding in the app
Used by recognizable brands including NatWest, BeautyCounter, and Tom Ford
Rated 4.5-4.7 stars across Capterra, G2, GetApp, and Software Advice per the company's site
Includes livestreaming, course monetization, and analytics without needing separate tools
cons: Entry-level plan starts at $399/month, higher than many community platforms
No free plan or published free trial listed on the pricing page
API access and unlimited courses are reserved for the top Branded App Pro tier
Organisation-level custom pricing requires a sales conversation

faqs.question: Does Disciple offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: The pricing page does not list a free trial; plans start at $399/month when billed annually.

faqs.question: Can I remove Disciple branding from my app?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Branded App Plus tier and above state there is "no mention of Disciple in app."

faqs.question: Does Disciple support course sales?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid tiers include monetizable courses with tiered video-minute allowances.

faqs.question: Is there an API?
faqs.answer: API access is included starting with the Branded App Pro plan.

use_cases.title: Creator membership apps
use_cases.description: Creators building a branded membership app with courses, groups, and livestreams.

use_cases.title: Enterprise brand communities
use_cases.description: Companies like NatWest running branded member apps for customers or employees.

features: Branded app in app stores and web app
No mention of Disciple in app

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Disciple?
paragraphs: Disciple is a platform that lets creators, brands, and organizations launch their own fully branded community app, listed in the iOS and Android app stores alongside a web version, without writing code.

The platform bundles groups, messaging, livestreaming, a content library, and course monetization into a single independently branded app, so operators are not dependent on third-party social platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Disciple is for
paragraphs: Disciple is aimed at creators, coaches, and established brands that want full ownership of their community experience. It has been used by organizations such as NatWest, BeautyCounter, and Tom Ford to run branded member apps.

name: Tribe Community Platform
skip_reason: tribe.so 301-redirects to bettermode.com. The destination site makes no reference to the 'Tribe' name anywhere on the page and presents itself solely as Bettermode — the product has been fully rebranded, so it no longer operates under the listed name.

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one community platform for creators to run paid communities with courses, events, and discussions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Skool is an all-in-one community platform where creators host paid communities, courses, and events, founded in 2019 and based in Los Angeles.

features.title: Community discussions
features.description: Unlimited members can post and discuss within a single community space on both plans.

features.title: Course hosting
features.description: Unlimited course and video content hosted directly inside the community.

features.title: Live calls
features.description: Unlimited live calls for classes, coaching, or community sessions.

features.title: Custom URL
features.description: Communities get a custom URL on both the Hobby and Pro plans.

features.title: Affiliate program
features.description: Built-in affiliate program to help members promote paid communities.

features.title: Tiered transaction fees
features.description: Transaction fee drops from 10% on Hobby to 2.9% on Pro as revenue scales.

pros: Simple flat two-plan pricing structure ($9 or $99/month)
Unlimited members, courses, videos, and live calls on both plans
Free trial available before committing to a paid plan
Backed by a partnership with Alex Hormozi (Skool Games, 2024)
cons: Hobby plan charges a 10% transaction fee on sales
Even the Pro plan retains a 2.9% transaction fee
No perpetually free community tier listed on the pricing page

faqs.question: What's the difference between Skool's Hobby and Pro plans?
faqs.answer: Both include unlimited members, courses, videos, and live calls; the difference is the transaction fee — 10% on Hobby ($9/month) versus 2.9% on Pro ($99/month).

faqs.question: Does Skool offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, both the Hobby and Pro plans offer a free trial before payment.

faqs.question: Who founded Skool?
faqs.answer: Skool was founded in 2019 by CEO Sam Ovens and CTO Daniel Kang.

faqs.question: Where is Skool based?
faqs.answer: Skool is headquartered in Los Angeles with around 30 employees, per its About page.

use_cases.title: Paid community and course business
use_cases.description: Creators bundling community access with paid courses and live calls in one product.

use_cases.title: Hobby and interest communities
use_cases.description: Hobbyists and experts starting a community around a shared interest before monetizing further.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Skool?
paragraphs: Skool is a community platform where creators, artists, hobbyists, and experts can build online communities around subscription memberships or one-time course purchases.
Founded in 2019 by Sam Ovens and Daniel Kang and headquartered in Los Angeles, Skool combines community discussions, course hosting, and live calls in a single product.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Skool is for
paragraphs: Skool targets creators and experts who want to monetize an audience through a paid community and courses, with a flat two-plan pricing structure based on transaction fees rather than feature gating.

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one monetized community platform combining membership management, courses, events, and payments.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Heartbeat is an all-in-one platform for monetized communities, combining courses, events, and payments, with plans from $49/month and a 14-day trial.

features.title: Community spaces
features.description: Channels, live rooms, discussions, and a member directory with personalized feeds.

features.title: Courses
features.description: Evergreen and cohort-based courses with video hosting, assignments, and progress tracking.

features.title: Events
features.description: Ticketed workshops and webinars with Zoom and calendar integration plus attendance tracking.

features.title: Payments
features.description: Membership tiers, subscriptions, installment plans, and affiliate programs built in.

features.title: Pulse AI
features.description: Automated community setup, including course creation, page generation, and workflow automation.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Stripe, Zapier, Zoom, Google Calendar, Notion, and Mailchimp.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Transaction fees decrease as plan tier increases, from 5% down to 1.25%
Recognized as a G2 Momentum Leader (Spring 2025) with High Performer badges
Reports processing $23M+ in member payments across 25,000+ communities
cons: Entry Build plan caps membership at 350 members
Charges its own transaction fee on top of Stripe's processing fees
White-label mobile app is limited to the top-tier Scale plan ($849/month)
API access requires the Grow plan or higher

faqs.question: How much does Heartbeat cost?
faqs.answer: Plans run Build ($49/month), Grow ($149/month), and Scale ($849/month), each with lower monthly pricing on annual billing.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is offered with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Heartbeat charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: Yes, on top of Stripe's processing fees, Heartbeat charges 5% (Build), 2.5% (Grow), or 1.25% (Scale).

faqs.question: What is Pulse AI?
faqs.answer: Heartbeat's built-in AI feature that automates community setup, including course creation, page generation, and workflow automation.

use_cases.title: Creator-led paid communities
use_cases.description: Creators consolidating membership, courses, and events into one branded site instead of multiple tools.

use_cases.title: Coaching and consulting programs
use_cases.description: Business coaches running cohort-based courses and ticketed events alongside a community.

features: Unlimited channels, courses, events, documents & landing pages

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Heartbeat?
paragraphs: Heartbeat is an all-in-one platform for creators and businesses to build monetized communities, consolidating membership management, course hosting, event coordination, and payment processing into a single branded website.
The platform reports over 25,000 communities built and more than $23M in member payments processed, with a built-in AI feature called Pulse AI that automates community setup.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Heartbeat is for
paragraphs: Heartbeat is built for content creators, course instructors, and business coaches who want to run community, courses, events, and payments from one place instead of stitching together separate tools.

enrichment.short_description: Consumer app for discovering local interest-based groups and coordinating in-person meetups, now owned by Bumble.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Geneva helps people find and join local interest-based groups for offline meetups, connecting members through messaging and event coordination.

features.title: Location-based group discovery
features.description: Browse groups by city, with examples highlighted in Los Angeles, New York, Dallas, and Boston.

features.title: Interest-specific communities
features.description: Communities organized around shared interests such as running, dining, film, and thrifting.

features.title: In-app messaging
features.description: Coordinate offline activities and meetups directly through group chat.

features.title: Web and app access
features.description: Available as a web app or as a downloadable app.

pros: Focused specifically on turning online groups into real offline meetups
Wide range of interest categories represented, from fitness to dining to film
Accessible via both a web app and a downloadable app
Now backed by Bumble Inc. following its acquisition of Geneva
cons: No pricing, plans, or monetization details published on the site
Founding year, headquarters, and team size are not disclosed
Future product direction is uncertain following the Bumble acquisition
Homepage highlights a limited set of city-based communities rather than global coverage

faqs.question: Is Geneva owned by Bumble?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bumble Inc.'s acquisition of Geneva has officially closed, per Geneva's website.

faqs.question: What does Geneva do?
faqs.answer: Geneva is a platform for finding local, interest-based groups and coordinating in-person meetups.

faqs.question: Can I use Geneva without downloading an app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Geneva can be opened directly in a browser in addition to its downloadable app.

faqs.question: What kinds of groups are on Geneva?
faqs.answer: Examples on the site include running clubs, dinner clubs, and film-focused communities in cities like New York and Boston.

use_cases.title: Finding local interest groups
use_cases.description: People looking to join existing city-based groups organized around a shared hobby or interest.

use_cases.title: Coordinating in-person meetups
use_cases.description: Group organizers using in-app messaging to plan and coordinate offline activities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Geneva?
paragraphs: Geneva describes itself as "the online place to find your offline people," connecting users to local, interest-based groups for in-person activities such as running clubs and dinner clubs.
The platform is accessible via a web app or a downloadable app, and Bumble Inc. has completed its acquisition of Geneva.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Geneva is for
paragraphs: Geneva is aimed at people who want to find and join existing local communities organized around a shared interest, and then coordinate real-world meetups through group messaging.

name: PeerBoard
skip_reason: peerboard.com 301-redirects to docebo.com/products/communities. PeerBoard has been acquired and folded into Docebo's Communities product, with no standalone 'PeerBoard' branding remaining on the destination site.

enrichment.short_description: Community software for discussion forums, Q&A, and knowledge bases used by enterprise support and membership teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Forumbee is community software offering discussion forums, Q&A, and knowledge bases for customer support, starting at $250/month.

features.title: Discussion forums
features.description: Peer-to-peer discussion spaces for members and customers.

features.title: Community Q&A
features.description: Customer-powered support through community question-and-answer threads.

features.title: Knowledge base
features.description: Publish and organize help content alongside community discussions.

features.title: Ideation tools
features.description: Collect and prioritize customer feedback and feature ideas.

features.title: Community analytics
features.description: Reports for tracking member engagement and community activity.

features.title: Gamification
features.description: Badges and leaderboards to encourage participation.

features.title: SSO on Enterprise
features.description: SAML SSO and Okta support included on the Enterprise plan.

pros: Used by recognizable organizations including Accenture, Kiva, Turnitin, and Girl Scouts
Combines forums, Q&A, knowledge base, and ideation in one product
Enterprise plan includes SAML SSO/Okta and a dedicated success manager on annual subscriptions
Custom "Build Your Own" plan available for larger enterprise needs
cons: No free plan or free trial mentioned on the pricing page
Entry Premium plan starts at $250/month and is capped at 2,000 members
Gamification module is an additional cost on top of the Enterprise plan
Add-ons such as custom themes and account setup carry significant extra fees

faqs.question: How much does Forumbee cost?
faqs.answer: The Premium plan is $250/month (5 admins, 2,000 members) and Enterprise is $750/month (20 admins, 20,000 members); a custom "Build Your Own" plan is also available.

faqs.question: Does Forumbee include a knowledge base?
faqs.answer: Yes, Knowledge Base is a standard feature included on all plans, alongside discussion forums, Q&A, and ideation.

faqs.question: Does Forumbee support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: SAML SSO and Okta support are included on the Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: Can I add more members to my plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, additional blocks of 5,000 members can be added for $100/month.

use_cases.title: Customer support communities
use_cases.description: Companies scaling support through peer-to-peer Q&A and a searchable knowledge base.

use_cases.title: Membership and advocacy organizations
use_cases.description: Mission-driven organizations building engagement and knowledge sharing among members.

features: Dedicated success manager with annual subscription
Extra admin users, members, and page views

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Forumbee?
paragraphs: Forumbee is community software that helps organizations build online communities and knowledge bases, combining discussion forums, community Q&A, a knowledge base, and ideation tools for collecting customer feedback.
The platform is used by organizations including Accenture, Kiva, Turnitin, Girl Scouts, Fox Entertainment, and Cambridge Judge Business School.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Forumbee is for
paragraphs: Forumbee targets companies scaling customer support, organizations focused on client relationships, and membership organizations that want peer-to-peer knowledge sharing alongside a formal knowledge base.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source forum software built on Laravel with a large community extension ecosystem.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Flarum is free, open-source forum software under the MIT license, built on Laravel with hundreds of community extensions.

features.title: Flexible editing
features.description: Lightweight Markdown editing plus an optional full WYSIWYG editor.

features.title: Notifications and digests
features.description: In-browser notifications and email digest options to keep members engaged.

features.title: Moderation controls
features.description: Group-based permissions and built-in flagging for community moderation.

features.title: Extension ecosystem
features.description: Hundreds of community-built extensions available, plus support for custom extension development.

features.title: Laravel foundation
features.description: Built on the Laravel PHP framework with MySQL, MariaDB, or PostgreSQL support.

features.title: Performance focus
features.description: Recent releases target N+1 query fixes and mobile optimization.

pros: Completely free and open-source under the MIT license
Used by organizations including giffgaff, Sunrise, and Swisscom
Large extension ecosystem for customization without forking core code
Actively developed, with recent releases focused on performance and mobile
cons: Self-hosting requires your own server and database setup
No official hosted or managed version mentioned on the site
Still at a release-candidate stage (2.0.0-rc.3) rather than a marked-stable 2.0 release

faqs.question: Is Flarum free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Flarum is free, open-source software released under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What is Flarum built on?
faqs.answer: Flarum is built on the Laravel PHP framework and supports MySQL, MariaDB, and PostgreSQL databases.

faqs.question: Can I extend Flarum's features?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are hundreds of community-built extensions, and custom extensions can also be developed.

faqs.question: Is Flarum production-ready?
faqs.answer: As of the current release (2.0.0-rc.3), Flarum describes itself as approaching a stable release with a focus on production-readiness.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted community forums
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting full control over a self-hosted discussion forum without licensing fees.

use_cases.title: Branded support and product forums
use_cases.description: Companies like giffgaff and Swisscom running branded support or discussion forums.
use_cases.audience: Companies running customer or product forums

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Flarum?
paragraphs: Flarum is open-source forum software built on Laravel, designed to be "beautiful, fast, and free," with extensibility as a core focus through a large ecosystem of community-built extensions.
It is released under the MIT license, supports MySQL, MariaDB, and PostgreSQL, and is used by organizations including giffgaff, Sunrise, and Swisscom.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Flarum is for
paragraphs: Flarum suits developers and community managers who want a self-hosted, free forum platform they can customize deeply through extensions and custom CSS, from small hobby forums to larger installations.

enrichment.short_description: Modern, mobile-first forum software available as open source or hosted plans starting at $20/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: NodeBB is mobile-first forum software with real-time posts and ActivityPub support, offered free open source or hosted from $20/month.

features.title: Mobile-first design
features.description: Responsive interface designed to work across all devices and screen sizes.

features.title: Real-time updates
features.description: Live streaming of topics, posts, and notifications as they happen.

features.title: Built-in chat
features.description: Instant messaging and social feed views alongside traditional forum threads.

features.title: Tag and category system
features.description: Flexible content organization with role-based permissions assignable per category.

features.title: ActivityPub support
features.description: Fediverse integration for connecting with the broader decentralized social web.

features.title: SEO optimization
features.description: Human-readable URLs and semantic HTML for search visibility.

features.title: GDPR compliance
features.description: NodeBB states it was the first forum software to achieve GDPR compliance.

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source option available
Hosted plans include a 14-day free trial
Real-time functionality, including live posts and notifications, beyond typical forum software
Used by established communities including Vivaldi, Opera, and MOZ
cons: Hosted trial requires a credit card upfront
Storage and pageview limits scale sharply with plan cost, from $20 to $750/month
Self-hosting the open-source version requires technical setup and ongoing maintenance

faqs.question: Is NodeBB free?
faqs.answer: NodeBB offers a free, self-hosted open-source version, plus paid hosted plans starting at $20/month.

faqs.question: Does NodeBB offer a free trial for hosted plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, hosted plans include a 14-day free trial, though a credit card is required.

faqs.question: What hosted plans does NodeBB offer?
faqs.answer: Starter ($20/month), Hamlet ($100/month), Village ($250/month), and City ($750/month), plus custom Enterprise pricing.

faqs.question: Does NodeBB support the fediverse?
faqs.answer: Yes, NodeBB includes ActivityPub support for fediverse integration.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted community forums
use_cases.description: Technical teams self-hosting the open-source version for full control over infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Managed forum hosting
use_cases.description: Businesses wanting a hosted forum without managing their own server, choosing a plan based on expected traffic.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is NodeBB?
paragraphs: NodeBB is modern forum software designed for mobile-first, real-time community discussions, offering built-in chat, ActivityPub fediverse support, and a role-based permission system by category.
It's available as free, self-hosted open-source software, as one-click hosted plans starting at $20/month with a 14-day free trial, or as a custom Enterprise solution.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who NodeBB is for
paragraphs: NodeBB serves technical teams that want to self-host for free, growing businesses that prefer a managed hosted plan, and larger organizations needing custom enterprise integrations. Established users include Vivaldi, Opera, and MOZ.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source forum software maintained by volunteers, in development since 2002.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: MyBB is free, open-source forum software maintained by volunteers since 2002, with plugins, themes, and community translations.

features.title: Plugins and themes
features.description: Hundreds of plugins and themes available for customizing forum functionality and appearance.

features.title: Community translations
features.description: The forum is available in 30+ languages through community-contributed translations.

features.title: Moderation tools
features.description: Tools for forum moderators and staff to manage discussions and members.

features.title: Admin dashboard
features.description: Full control panel for managing forum settings and users.

features.title: Private messaging
features.description: Members can participate in private conversations with other registered users.

features.title: Template editor
features.description: Built-in template editing for design customization without external tools.

pros: Completely free and open source, maintained by volunteers
Long track record, in development since 2002
Large plugin and theme ecosystem
Translated into 30+ languages by the community
cons: Relies on volunteer maintenance rather than a dedicated commercial support team
Self-hosting requires setting up and maintaining your own server
No official hosted or managed version mentioned on the site

faqs.question: Is MyBB free?
faqs.answer: Yes, MyBB is free and open-source software, maintained by volunteers with optional donations.

faqs.question: How long has MyBB been around?
faqs.answer: MyBB has been in development since 2002, per its website.

faqs.question: Can MyBB be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes, hundreds of plugins and themes are available, along with a built-in template editor.

faqs.question: Is MyBB available in languages other than English?
faqs.answer: Yes, community translations are available in more than 30 languages.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted discussion forums
use_cases.description: Community organizers self-hosting a free forum without licensing costs.

use_cases.title: Multilingual communities
use_cases.description: Communities needing a forum available in languages other than English through community translations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MyBB?
paragraphs: MyBB is free, open-source forum software that has been in development since 2002, maintained by volunteers and supported through community donations.
It powers a wide range of community forums, offering plugins, themes, a template editor, and moderation tools out of the box.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MyBB is for
paragraphs: MyBB is aimed at community organizers and forum administrators who want a free, self-hosted forum with a mature plugin and theme ecosystem, including support for over 30 languages via community translations.
